qa_pairs.question: How do you connect Visual Studio Code to a remote host?
qa_pairs.answer: To connect Visual Studio Code to a remote host, open the Command Palette (by pressing Ctrl+Shift+P on Windows/Linux or Cmd+Shift+P on macOS), type "remote", and then select "Connect to Host...". Finally, choose the desired host (remote system) and confirm.

qa_pairs.question: Which type of system node will Visual Studio Code connect to initially when establishing a remote connection?
qa_pairs.answer: When you establish a remote connection with Visual Studio Code using the standard connection process, you will initially be connected to a login node.

qa_pairs.question: What actions should be avoided when Visual Studio Code is connected to a login node?
qa_pairs.answer: When Visual Studio Code is connected to a login node, you must not test, debug, or run your code directly on it, as this can lead to performance issues or system disruptions.
